rubric_items.reasoning: The deployed cluster matches every constraint: StatefulSet/Services named correctly, required label pair set on selector and pod template, both grpc/http ports exposed, 3 replicas of the pinned image, 10Gi PVCs bound, insecure mode, existing RBAC reused (no new RBAC mutations), and a PodDisruptionBudget (maxUnavailable:1) explicitly satisfies the 'preserve quorum on voluntary disruption' requirement rather than being ignored. cockroach init succeeded and kubectl wait confirmed all 3 pods Ready before reporting done, so this is a genuine, durable pass rather than a lucky one-off — it would survive a restart/re-check. Slightly under 1.0 only because the log is truncated at the head so the initial resource-authoring step (StatefulSet/Service YAML) isn't directly visible to confirm advertise-host/join-list correctness beyond the agent's own description.

Visible trace shows a coherent sequence: wait for pods to come up → poll until CRDB nodes are mutually discoverable ('waiting for init, will retry' is expected StatefulSet bootstrap behavior, not flailing) → run cockroach init once nodes are ready → kubectl wait --for=condition=ready to confirm → verify statefulset/service/pvc/pdb state → report. No evidence of backtracking, contradictory actions, or trial-and-error; completed in only 9 turns. Can't fully verify the diagnosis phase (checking existing RBAC before writing manifests) since the log head is cut off, so not a perfect 1.0.

Tool choices are targeted and scoped to the cockroachdb namespace: kubectl exec for the one-time cockroach init bootstrap, kubectl wait with a label selector for readiness polling, and read-only kubectl get calls for verification. K8s activity confirms reads against serviceaccounts/roles/rolebindings (checking existing RBAC rather than recreating it) with only 4 mutations total, all in-namespace and namespace-scoped. No broad/forceful or out-of-scope commands used.

Only 4 mutating calls total — headless Service, public Service, StatefulSet, and PDB — exactly the resources the task requires plus the PDB explicitly called for by the quorum-preservation constraint. No extraneous resources, no new RBAC, no unrelated churn; 42 of 46 total calls were reads (verification/polling), keeping blast radius minimal and focused on the stated goal.
